meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Fix No Version Control for Students
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how to use SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to master version control and ship your first professional project.
content.answer_block: Stop manually copying folders. A SaaS starter kit provides a production-ready codebase with Git integrated by default. By pairing Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you enforce version control from line one, ensuring your student projects are scalable, professional, and audit-ready for future employers.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours crafting logic, only to realize your project is a fragmented mess of 'final_v2' folders. This is the 'no version control' trap—the silent killer of student developer momentum. It turns your hard work into a graveyard of lost progress, making it impossible to iterate or showcase your skills to recruiters.

But the industry doesn't work in silos. Professional SaaS development relies on robust starter kits that bake version control into the foundation. By adopting these tools, you aren't just saving your code; you are building a professional workflow that mirrors what senior engineers use at top-tier tech companies.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean Git history before writing a single line of application code to ensure every change is tracked.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust SaaS boilerplate structure that includes a .gitignore file, a README, and a git-ready repository architecture.

steps.title: Configure Version Control Hooks
steps.description: Automate linting and formatting on every commit to maintain code quality standards automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up Husky and lint-staged in the project to ensure code is formatted before any commit is accepted.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install husky and configure pre-commit hooks'

steps.title: Implement Feature Branching
steps.description: Learn to isolate new features in branches to prevent breaking the main production-ready codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a guide for a student on how to use git flow branching for a SaaS project, specifically for auth and database features.

steps.title: Automate Documentation via Commits
steps.description: Use conventional commits to auto-generate changelogs, making your portfolio projects look highly professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a commit template that forces conventional commit standards (feat, fix, docs, chore) for this SaaS project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup commitlint for conventional commits'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Setup
steps.description: Connect your repository to a CI tool to test your code automatically every time you push to GitHub.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs tests and builds the project on every push to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always commit in atomic chunks; one logical change per commit makes debugging 10x faster.
Use 'git stash' when you need to switch tasks urgently without committing half-finished code.
Never hardcode environment variables; use a .env.example file in your starter kit so your Git history stays clean.
Tag your releases (v1.0.0) to mark significant milestones for your portfolio reviewers.
Learn to squash commits before merging to keep your main branch history clean and readable for recruiters.
content.next_steps: After completing your first commit, push your project to a public GitHub repository and add a detailed README to showcase your work to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to stop losing work and start shipping like a pro. Don't let your code sit idle on your hard drive. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
